evals.prompt: Run sync-docs. Live kubectl shows context 'harumi-prod-eks' with ingress domain 'app.harumi.io'. Live AWS shows: aws sts get-caller-identity returns account 987654321098, aws eks describe-cluster returns endpoint 'https://NEWENDPOINT.gr7.us-east-1.eks.amazonaws.com', and aws ecr describe-repositories returns registry URI '987654321098.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'. The checked-in harumi.yaml has context 'eks-prod', domain 'harumi.io', account '123456789012', and registry '123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'. Update the repo.
evals.expected_output: Detects drift across both AWS-sourced fields (account ID, ECR registry URI, EKS endpoint) and Kubernetes-sourced fields (context name, ingress domain). Treats live AWS and live Kubernetes state as the source of truth for their respective surfaces. Regenerates harumi.yaml using the live-observed values from both sources. Writes the file silently. Summary shows both sources as live.
expectations: Identifies AWS-side drift: account ID changed from '123456789012' to '987654321098'
Identifies AWS-side drift: ECR registry URI changed to '987654321098.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
Identifies Kubernetes-side drift: cluster context changed from 'eks-prod' to 'harumi-prod-eks'
Identifies Kubernetes-side drift: ingress domain changed from 'harumi.io' to 'app.harumi.io'
Regenerates harumi.yaml using live AWS values (new account ID, new registry URI) and live Kubernetes values (new context, new domain)
Writes harumi.yaml silently without prompting the user (it is a generated artifact)
Does NOT retain any of the stale values from the original checked-in harumi.yaml
Summary shows 'AWS=[live]' and 'Kubernetes=[live]' (or equivalent wording) for the harumi.yaml source line

evals.prompt: Run sync-docs. AWS CLI is available: aws sts get-caller-identity returns account '123456789012', aws eks list-clusters returns 'eks-prod', aws ecr describe-repositories returns registry '123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'. However, kubectl is not installed. Sync what you can.
evals.expected_output: Uses live AWS data for AWS-sourced fields. Reports that live Kubernetes drift could not be verified (kubectl unavailable) and falls back to repo data for Kubernetes-sourced fields. Does NOT invent cluster contexts, ingress domains, or namespace state. Summary shows AWS as live and Kubernetes as unavailable/repo-only, with an explicit note that live drift could not be verified for Kubernetes.
expectations: Uses live AWS state (account ID, EKS cluster name, ECR registry URI) for AWS-sourced fields in harumi.yaml
Does NOT fail or stop because kubectl is unavailable — degrades gracefully for the Kubernetes surface
Explicitly reports 'live drift could not be verified for Kubernetes' (or equivalent) in the response or summary
Falls back to repo data (Terraform files, K8s manifests) for Kubernetes-sourced fields such as cluster context names and ingress domains
Does NOT invent Kubernetes cluster contexts, ingress domains, namespace lists, or workload state
Summary shows AWS as available/live and Kubernetes as unavailable, with separate per-source reporting
